title: Daily Briefing — Jun 02
headline: Tech leads, but under the hood we’re seeing laggards and defensive rotation — flows aren’t confirming the euphoria.
body: Right, so S&P (SPY $758.57) and Nasdaq (QQQ $743.32) are grinding higher, but it's not the broad market strength everyone wants to see. VIX is behaving, trending below its 20-day (16.05 vs 17.14), and Fear & Greed has cooled off to 57.1 — no longer extreme, but still complacent territory.

Sector rotation is messy: XLK (Tech) is the sole leader, with SMA20 at $179.51 and price way above at $196.66. But financials (XLF $51.17) have triggered support breaches — below weekly SMA200 ($52.27) is a real canary moment, and energy, discretionary, and comms all lagging. Money is starting to drift into materials (XLB $51.21) and industrials (XLI $173.45), but healthcare, staples, and utilities are only 'improving,' not out of the woods.

Contrarian flags: Retail still chasing tech (AAPL, NVDA, MSFT) while financials break, so be suspicious. Weekly closes below key MAs (GOOGL, XLC, XLRE, ADSK, S, KMB) are triggering exits per the 20 MA rule. Don’t chase: abundance mindset. Macro flows aren’t showing panic, but if bonds freak out (watch HYG/LQD), reduce exposure fast.
callout_body:
SPY: $743.26 (SMA20) — must hold for trend
XLF: $51.17 (price) vs $52.27 (SMA200) — support broken, risk up
GOOGL: $371.29 (price) vs $390.09 (SMA20) — bearish trigger
AAPL: $310.19 (price) vs $300.46 (SMA20) — breakout zone
HYG: $79.87 (approaching SMA20 $79.55) — monitor closely
Fear & Greed: 57.1 — no edge, but getting complacent
action_items: Exit or reduce XLF, XLRE, XLC exposure — support breaks and lagging signals
Watch HYG/LQD for macro breakdown — first defense if bonds roll over
closing_quote: If financials are sick, it's not bullish — patience, react, don't predict.
